skill.name: migrate-to-shoehorn
skill.description: Migrate TypeScript test files from `as` assertions to @total-typescript/shoehorn helpers for partial test data.
skill.summary: Convert TypeScript test assertions to Shoehorn helpers for clearer partial test data.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution, but these are TypeScript examples and inline code spans. The confirmed external commands are limited to npm installation, grep search, and type-check verification guidance. No prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: External Command Guidance Requires User Review
low_findings.description: The skill asks users to install @total-typescript/shoehorn and run grep or type-check commands. These are normal migration steps, but they modify dependencies or inspect local files, so users should review commands before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command text is directly present in SKILL.md and matches the skill purpose. The commands are ordinary development commands, so the issue is a low operational risk, not malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick findings correspond to markdown inline code and TypeScript fenced examples. They do not execute code and do not instruct the agent to bypass security controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line inspection shows markdown formatting and TypeScript migration examples rather than Ruby backtick execution. There is no executable Ruby code or shell interpolation in the skill file.

low_findings.title: High-Risk Scanner Labels Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography label points to the word `as` inside the description, and the reconnaissance label points to a test comment. No cryptographic operation or system reconnaissance instruction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain skill metadata and an explanatory test comment. They provide no semantic evidence of cryptography, host discovery, or malicious reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dependency Installation Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends installing @total-typescript/shoehorn with npm. This is expected for the migration, but it changes project dependencies.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npm command is explicit and directly tied to installing the required package. It is not hidden or obfuscated, but it still performs a dependency change.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Search Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow recommends grep to find TypeScript test assertions. This reads local project files but does not send data outside the environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The grep command is explicit and scoped to test and spec TypeScript files. No network destination or secret handling appears on the referenced line.

content.value_statement: TypeScript tests often use unsafe `as` assertions to build partial test objects. This skill guides conversion to @total-typescript/shoehorn helpers for clearer and safer test data.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to replace `as Type` with Shoehorn helpers.
Maps partial test objects to `fromPartial()` usage.
Maps intentional invalid test data to `fromAny()` usage.
Shows how to add Shoehorn imports in TypeScript tests.
Provides a workflow for locating test assertion patterns.
limitations: Applies only to test code, not production code.
Does not provide an automated codemod or parser.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Test Fixtures
use_cases.description: Convert large mocked objects into focused partial test data with clearer intent.

use_cases.title: Reduce Unsafe Assertions
use_cases.description: Replace routine test assertions with Shoehorn helpers that preserve stronger typing.

use_cases.title: Standardize Migration Reviews
use_cases.description: Apply consistent rules when reviewing test changes that remove double assertions.

prompt_templates.title: Find Basic Replacements
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner migration of simple test assertions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my TypeScript test file and identify `as Type` assertions that should become Shoehorn `fromPartial()` calls.

prompt_templates.title: Migrate One Test File
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert a single test file after Shoehorn is available.
prompt_templates.prompt: Migrate this test file from `as` assertions to @total-typescript/shoehorn helpers and explain each replacement briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Invalid Test Data
prompt_templates.scenario: Tests intentionally pass wrong shapes or values.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these tests for `as unknown as` patterns and replace intentional invalid data with the right Shoehorn helper.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Repository Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced migration across many test files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a phased migration plan for replacing unsafe test assertions with Shoehorn across this TypeScript repository.

output_examples.input: A test builds a large request object only to provide a body identifier.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a partial test object, adds the Shoehorn import, and explains why the unused fields can be removed.

output_examples.input: A test uses a double assertion to pass an intentionally invalid value.
output_examples.output: The assistant classifies it as invalid test data and recommends the helper intended for deliberately unsafe shapes.

output_examples.input: A team wants to remove assertions from many test files.
output_examples.output: The assistant produces a migration checklist covering dependency installation, search strategy, replacement rules, and type-check verification.

best_practices: Use Shoehorn only in test files.
Run the project type check after each migration batch.
Keep intentional invalid data clearly described in the test name or assertion.
anti_patterns: Do not use Shoehorn helpers in production code.
Do not replace meaningful domain builders when they express important behavior.
Do not hide real type errors by converting every assertion without review.

faq.question: What does this skill migrate?
faq.answer: It helps migrate TypeScript test assertions from `as` patterns to @total-typescript/shoehorn helpers.

faq.question: Is this for production code?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly says Shoehorn should be used only in test code.

faq.question: Which helper replaces normal partial objects?
faq.answer: `fromPartial()` is used when the test only needs part of an object shape.

faq.question: Which helper handles intentionally wrong data?
faq.answer: `fromAny()` is used when a test deliberately passes data with the wrong type.

faq.question: Does the skill run the migration automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides migration patterns and workflow guidance, not an automated codemod.

faq.question: What should I check after migrating?
faq.answer: Run the project type check and review tests that intentionally use invalid data.
